courses.title: Rotating Equipment
courses.summary: The machines this pathway exists for: pumps, compressors, seals, bearings, couplings, and gearboxes — theory from the fluids and design courses, failure modes from the field, all eight Core-60 lessons in one course.
taught_from: SMRP CMRP framework for practice content

lessons.scope: Euler head to real head: the H–Q curve derived, then read like an instrument — BEP, shutoff, runout, and efficiency contours. Power and affinity laws with their honest limits. The condensed example: re-rating a pump for a speed change without leaving the curve's validity.
lessons.src: Çengel & Cimbala, Ch. 14; supplementary video: The Efficient Engineer — pumps
preview: Affinity laws: speed drops 10% — what happens to flow, head, and power?
Where on the H–Q curve does efficiency peak, and what goes wrong running far out on the curve?
Why is sustained operation at shutoff destructive when a brief excursion is survivable?

lessons.scope: System resistance built from static head plus friction; the operating point as intersection. NPSH available derived from the installation, NPSH required read from the vendor, and the margin between them defended. Cavitation's physics, sound, and damage signature — and why throttling a suction valve is a firing offense.
lessons.src: Çengel & Cimbala, Ch. 8, 14; supplementary video: The Efficient Engineer — pumps
preview: NPSHa = 6 m against NPSHr = 4.5 m: verdict, and what margin does good practice want?
Why is a 5 m suction lift of 60 °C water already in trouble before any calculation?
Slightly throttling the discharge quiets cavitation noise: explain the mechanism.

lessons.scope: Gear, screw, diaphragm, and reciprocating pumps: flow nearly independent of pressure, and the relief-valve consequence that follows. Pulsation and its dampeners. Where PD pumps replace centrifugals — viscosity, metering, and low-flow/high-head duty.
preview: Why must a PD pump never start against a closed discharge without a relief path?
At fixed speed, what changes gear-pump delivery as pressure rises, and by what mechanism?
Why does metering duty default to PD pumps over centrifugals?

lessons.scope: Centrifugal and reciprocating compressors with the thermodynamics attached: polytropic work, discharge temperature, and capacity control. Surge — the phenomenon, the map, and the anti-surge loop. Air and process compression duties across the six anchor plants.
lessons.src: NPTEL: Applied Thermodynamics content; Çengel & Cimbala
preview: What sets reciprocating-compressor discharge temperature, and what limits pressure ratio per stage?
Define surge in a centrifugal compressor in one sentence.
Why does intercooling between stages save power? Tie it to the T–s picture.

lessons.scope: Seal anatomy: faces, secondary seals, springs, and the fluid film that does the real work. Seal plans in outline — flush, quench, and dual-seal logic. Failure reading: heat checking, coking, dry running. The single highest-value component lesson for a process-plant maintenance engineer.
lessons.src: SMRP framework; NPTEL rotating machinery content; supplementary video: The Efficient Engineer
preview: Name a mechanical seal's two sealing interfaces — which rotates and which is static?
Why can thirty seconds of dry running end a seal that survived three years?
What does a flush plan physically deliver to the seal faces, and against which failure modes?

lessons.scope: Rolling-element and journal bearings from the design course, now in service: fits, internal clearance, lubrication regimes, and the L10 promise versus installed reality. Grease quantity and interval arithmetic. Failure atlas: spalling, fluting, false brinelling — each traced to its cause.
lessons.src: NPTEL: Machine Design content; SMRP framework; supplementary video: The Efficient Engineer
preview: L10 is 40,000 h at design load; load rises 26%. Estimate the new L10 for ball bearings.
Why is over-greasing a bearing as damaging as starvation?
Fluting on a bearing raceway: cause, and which drive technology made it epidemic?

lessons.scope: Coupling types and what each forgives; shaft alignment by rim-and-face and reverse-dial logic with tolerances stated in real units; field balancing in one and two planes. Thermal growth targets. The precision-maintenance trio that removes the two commonest vibration sources before they start.
lessons.src: NPTEL: Theory of Machines content; SMRP framework
preview: What do rim-and-face and reverse-dial methods both actually measure between two shafts?
Give the physical mechanism that puts misalignment's signature at 2× running speed.
Between balance grades G6.3 and G2.5, what changes physically on the rotor?

lessons.scope: Industrial gearbox architectures; loads and life from the design course compressed into inspection practice. The failure atlas: micropitting, macropitting, scuffing, tooth bending fatigue — with the oil-analysis and vibration signatures of each. Backlash and contact-pattern checks at rebuild.
lessons.src: NPTEL: Machine Design content; SMRP framework
preview: Compute gear-mesh frequency for a 23-tooth pinion at 1,475 rpm.
Micropitting versus macropitting: which lubrication property is implicated first, and why?
Sidebands around GMF spaced at output-shaft speed: what are they telling you?

lessons.scope: Fan laws and curves; static versus total pressure; balance and belt issues as the routine failure diet of cooling and ventilation duty in Gulf summer. Steam and gas turbines in outline: stage energy conversion recalled from thermodynamics, governing and protection systems, and steam quality's blade consequences. The rest of the rotating fleet, respected from a maintenance distance.
lessons.src: Çengel & Cimbala, Ch. 14; NPTEL: Applied Thermodynamics
preview: State the fan laws and what a 10% speed cut buys in power.
What does overspeed protection interrupt, and how fast must it act?

lessons.scope: Foundations, grouting, and soft foot; piping strain as the silent misalignment source. Commissioning checks and baseline data capture. The installation quality that decides the first five years of vibration history.
preview: What does soft foot do to a freshly aligned machine?
Why does piping strain masquerade as misalignment?

lessons.scope: A staged failed-pump scenario: history, vibration data, oil results, and teardown photographs provided. Produce the failure analysis and corrective actions. The course's eight core lessons deployed as one investigation.
preview: In the failure scenario, which evidence stream contradicted the first hypothesis?
What corrective action addressed cause rather than symptom?

courses.title: Asset Integrity Management
courses.summary: Keeping contained energy contained: integrity management systems, risk-based inspection, fitness-for-service thinking, and the inspection programs for static equipment — the asset integrity engineer's course.
taught_from: NPTEL: reliability and corrosion course content (nptel.ac.in)

lessons.scope: Integrity as the discipline over static equipment: vessels, piping, tanks, relief systems. Loss-of-containment consequence logic. The role's boundaries against maintenance and inspection functions.
lessons.src: NPTEL reliability content; industry framework survey
preview: What equipment classes does integrity own, and what consequence unites them?
Where does integrity end and maintenance begin?

lessons.scope: The degradation bestiary organized for static equipment: thinning, cracking, embrittlement, mechanical damage. Susceptibility mapped from process conditions. The corrosion course operationalized into an integrity register.
preview: Sort thinning, cracking, and embrittlement by inspectability.
Why does susceptibility mapping precede any inspection plan?

lessons.scope: Visual, UT thickness, shear-wave, and profile radiography deployed on vessels and piping. CML selection — where to measure so the data means something. Inspection effectiveness graded honestly.
preview: Why does CML selection decide whether thickness data means anything?
What does inspection effectiveness grading admit about methods?

lessons.scope: Probability and consequence of failure combined into inspection priority. Interval setting from remaining life and confidence. RBI as budget allocation defended with arithmetic.
preview: What two axes drive RBI priority, and what data feeds each?
How does RBI defend an interval extension to a regulator?

lessons.scope: The engineering question behind FFS: is the flawed equipment safe to run, and for how long? Corrosion allowance and minimum thickness logic; crack-like flaws flagged to specialists. When to call the assessment versus replace.
preview: What question does FFS answer that inspection cannot?
When does a wall-thinning finding demand assessment versus rerate versus replace?

lessons.scope: Relief philosophy: every credible overpressure case owns a device. PSV testing intervals and pre-test capture of as-found condition. The protective systems audit trail regulators read first.
preview: Why must every credible overpressure case map to a device?
What does as-found PSV test data feed?

lessons.scope: Piping classes, circuits, and injection-point programs; dead legs and small-bore vulnerability. Support and vibration walkdowns. Piping as the plant's largest and least-loved asset.
preview: Why are dead legs disproportionately dangerous?
What makes small-bore connections a fatigue problem?

lessons.scope: Atmospheric tank anatomy: shell, bottom, roof; settlement and corrosion patterns. Internal inspection logic and intervals. Floor scanning and the repair decisions it triggers.
preview: Why does tank bottom corrosion hide from external inspection?
What triggers an internal tank inspection?

lessons.scope: CUI's mechanism, temperature bands, and moisture pathways. Inspection strategies from stripping to screening. The Gulf coastal plant's standing CUI battle, budgeted properly.
preview: Name CUI's moisture pathways and the design details that feed them.
Why does screening precede stripping in CUI programs?

lessons.scope: Thickness data management, corrosion-rate calculation, and remaining-life projections. Overdue-inspection and temporary-repair KPIs. The integrity dashboard a plant manager should demand.
preview: Turn thickness history into a corrosion rate: what checks the number?
Which integrity KPIs would a plant manager read weekly?

lessons.scope: For a small unit: damage-mechanism register, CML plan, inspection schedule, and KPI set assembled into one integrity plan. The document set of the asset integrity engineer's first 90 days.
preview: In the unit plan, which damage mechanism dominated the register?
What did the KPI set watch that inspection alone would miss?

courses.title: Condition Monitoring & Predictive Maintenance
courses.summary: Reading machines before they fail: PdM strategy on P–F economics, vibration analysis from sensor to spectrum diagnosis, thermography, oil analysis, and ultrasound — all eight Core-60 lessons, the vibration analyst's foundation.
taught_from: NPTEL: Mechanical Vibrations (prerequisite); ISO 18436-aligned practice content

lessons.scope: Condition monitoring justified from the P–F curve: detection windows, inspection intervals at a fraction of P–F, and the money mathematics of caught-early versus run-to-wreck. Technology-to-failure-mode mapping as the program design act. The strategy lesson that keeps PdM from becoming gadget collecting.
lessons.src: SMRP CMRP framework; video: Mobius Institute
preview: A failure mode has a 4-month P–F interval: make the economic case for a monthly route.
Match technologies to failure modes: bearing spall, misalignment, hot electrical joint, degraded oil.
Why is owning an analyzer not the same as having a PdM program? Name the missing pieces.

lessons.scope: Amplitude, frequency, and phase as the analyst's three currencies; displacement, velocity, and acceleration with their frequency emphases and unit conversions. Overall levels and ISO-style zone thinking versus spectral analysis. The vibration course's theory recast into measurement language.
lessons.src: NPTEL: Mechanical Vibrations; ISO 18436-aligned content; video: Mobius Institute — vibration
preview: Convert 4 mm/s RMS at 100 Hz to a displacement amplitude — order of magnitude.
Why velocity for general severity, acceleration for bearings and gears, displacement for low-speed machines?
What does a commissioning baseline buy that absolute ISO-zone limits cannot?

lessons.scope: Accelerometer mounting and its frequency ceiling; Fmax, resolution, averaging, and windowing chosen deliberately. The FFT's gifts and traps — leakage, aliasing already met in Electronics. A measurement setup specified for a motor-pump train as the condensed example.
lessons.src: NPTEL: Mechanical Vibrations; signal content from ELX 205; video: Mobius Institute — vibration
preview: With Fmax 1,000 Hz and 1,600 lines, what is the spectral resolution per line?
Why does a loose magnet mount silently cap your usable Fmax?
What does a Hanning window trade away, and what does it buy?

lessons.t: Spectrum analysis I: unbalance, misalignment, looseness
lessons.scope: The first diagnostic trio: 1× dominant radial for unbalance; 2× and axial content for misalignment; harmonic families and raised floors for looseness. Phase analysis as the disambiguator. Case spectra read line by line, the way the certification practicals do.
lessons.src: ISO 18436-aligned content; NPTEL: Mechanical Vibrations; video: Mobius Institute — vibration
preview: Dominant 1× radial, little axial, stable phase: unbalance or misalignment — and what confirms it?
A harmonic forest 1×–10× over a raised floor: which fault family, and what mechanical check follows?
Why is phase measured before any field balancing attempt?

lessons.t: Spectrum analysis II: bearings and gears
lessons.scope: Rolling-element defect frequencies computed from geometry; the enveloping/demodulation technique and why early bearing damage hides from ordinary spectra. Gear mesh frequencies and sidebands as the gearbox's confession. Severity staging from first detection to removal decision.
lessons.src: ISO 18436-aligned content; video: Mobius Institute — vibration
preview: BPFO factor 3.58 at 1,480 rpm: what frequency line do you hunt?
Why does early bearing damage appear in enveloped spectra long before ordinary velocity spectra?
In a gearbox spectrum, sideband spacing around GMF identifies what?

lessons.scope: Radiation physics from the heat-transfer course made practical: emissivity, reflection, and why shiny metal lies to the camera. Electrical and mechanical survey practice; delta-T severity conventions. The condensed example: an MCC survey with three findings graded and reported.
lessons.src: Çengel & Ghajar, Ch. 12 foundations; ISO 18436-aligned practice; video: Mobius Institute
preview: A shiny bus bar with emissivity ~0.2 reads 45 °C: why might it be far hotter, and what fixes the reading?
Why must electrical thermography surveys run under meaningful load?
One phase of a three-phase connection runs 25 K hotter than its siblings: severity logic?

lessons.scope: The three questions of oil analysis — fluid health, contamination, machine wear — with the tests behind each: viscosity, TAN, particle counts, elements, ferrography in outline. Sampling technique as the program's real quality gate. Reading a gearbox report against its history.
lessons.src: SMRP framework; tribology content from MNT 306; video: Mobius Institute
preview: Viscosity up 20% and TAN doubled: what has happened to the oil, and what typically drove it?
Iron and chromium rising together in a gearbox sample: what does the pairing suggest?
Why does the sampling point decide whether an oil-analysis trend means anything?

lessons.scope: Airborne and structure-borne ultrasound for leaks, bearings, and electrical faults; motor current analysis in outline for rotor-bar and load diagnostics. Building PdM routes: what, where, how often, and who closes the loop. The program-assembly lesson that turns four technologies into one system.
lessons.src: SMRP CMRP framework; video: Mobius Institute
preview: What makes a 40 kHz ultrasound sensor find leaks your ears cannot?
In motor current analysis, broken rotor bars appear as sidebands spaced by what quantity?
Name three consistency disciplines that keep a monthly route's data comparable.

lessons.scope: A dozen anonymized cases across the four technologies read cold, then debriefed. Differential diagnosis practice: competing explanations ranked. The pattern library a working analyst carries.
preview: In the case marathon, which two cases shared symptoms but not causes?
What does differential diagnosis discipline prevent?

lessons.scope: Baselines, alarm limits, and trend curation; false-alarm discipline. Integrating PdM findings into the CMMS work flow. The administrative half that makes the technical half matter.
preview: Why do alarm limits need curation as machines age?
What closes the loop between a PdM finding and the CMMS?

lessons.scope: For a given equipment list: criticality-based technology assignment, routes, intervals, and KPI set, costed and staffed. The deliverable a reliability engineer presents to justify the program. Course capstone.
lessons.src: SMRP CMRP framework; video: Mobius Institute
preview: Which criticality tier got online monitoring in your design, and why?
How did you cost-justify the route frequencies?

courses.title: SCADA, DCS & IIoT
courses.summary: The data layer above the machines: SCADA architecture, historians, OEE from real signals, and the industrial internet — where production data becomes the numbers management runs the plant by.

lessons.scope: RTUs/PLCs, servers, and clients; polling versus report-by-exception. Redundancy patterns. SCADA versus DCS versus PLC+HMI — the taxonomy settled.
preview: Polling versus report-by-exception: what does each cost?
Where does redundancy belong in a SCADA architecture?

lessons.scope: Tags, scan classes, and deadbands; compression and its analytical costs. Time-series storage and retrieval. The historian as the plant's memory — and its garbage-in problem.
preview: What does deadband compression discard, and when does that bite analysis?
Why is the historian only as good as its tag metadata?

lessons.scope: Availability, performance, and quality computed from real machine states and counts — with the state-model design that decides whether OEE means anything. Loss trees beneath the top number. The condensed example: an OEE calculation built from a filling line's PLC signals, honestly handling changeovers and micro-stops.
lessons.src: NPTEL: Industrial Automation and Control; Groover, automation chapters
preview: Availability 90%, performance 95%, quality 99%: compute OEE.
A 10-minute changeover is logged as running-at-zero-rate: which OEE factor lies, and how does the state model fix it?
Why do sub-30-second micro-stops vanish from manual logs yet dominate performance loss?

lessons.scope: Alarm rationalization: priorities, floods, and standing-alarm hygiene. Metrics for alarm-system health. The control-room human factors this decides.
preview: What does an alarm flood do to operators, and what rationalization fixes?
Name two health metrics of an alarm system.

lessons.scope: OPC UA as the interoperability workhorse; MQTT for telemetry. Mapping the automation stack's data paths. Where integration projects actually stall.
preview: What does OPC UA standardize that drivers never did?
Why is MQTT's publish-subscribe model suited to telemetry?

lessons.scope: Wireless condition sensors, gateways, and edge processing. Battery, bandwidth, and placement realities. Retrofit monitoring for brownfield plants — the honest use case.
preview: What decides wireless sensor placement besides signal?
Why is retrofit monitoring the honest IIoT use case?

lessons.scope: Permanently installed sensors, protection versus prediction, and trip logic on critical trains. Proximity probes on journal-bearing machines. When online systems earn their cost.
preview: Protection versus prediction systems: what does each shutdown philosophy imply?
Why do journal-bearing machines carry proximity probes rather than accelerometers?

lessons.t: Dashboards and reporting that change behavior
lessons.scope: KPI design: leading versus lagging, owner versus audience. Dashboard sins: vanity metrics and unactionable charts. Production meetings run from data with provenance.
preview: Leading versus lagging KPIs: give one of each for maintenance.
What makes a dashboard change behavior rather than decorate?

lessons.t: Analytics and anomaly detection in outline
lessons.scope: Statistical baselines and simple models on historian data; what 'AI for PdM' can and cannot promise today. Pilot design with success criteria. Skepticism as an engineering instrument.
preview: What baseline must precede any anomaly-detection pilot?
Name two failure modes of 'AI for PdM' projects.

lessons.scope: Network zones and conduits; patching and backup discipline for control systems. Remote vendor access governed. The security posture a data project must not degrade.
preview: What do zones and conduits segment, and against what?
Why are control-system backups tested restores, not stored files?

lessons.scope: From a provided historian extract: build state logic, compute OEE, find the top three losses, and present the case for one improvement. Data engineering in production clothing. Course capstone.
preview: In the data project, which loss surprised you once states were honest?
What single improvement did the data justify?

courses.title: Lean & Six Sigma
courses.summary: Waste and variation hunted systematically: value-stream thinking, flow and pull, SMED, and the DMAIC toolkit — the production engineer's improvement operating system, statistics included.
taught_from: NPTEL: quality and operations management courses (nptel.ac.in)

lessons.scope: Value versus waste; the seven wastes spotted on a real line walk. Flow interrupted as the master symptom. Lean as engineering, stripped of ceremony.
preview: Name the seven wastes with one maintenance-shop example each.
Why is flow interruption the master symptom?

lessons.scope: Current-state mapping with data boxes and lead-time ladders. Future-state design rules. The map as an argument for specific, dated changes.
preview: What belongs in a data box on a current-state map?
Why does the lead-time ladder embarrass most processes?

lessons.scope: Batch-size effects derived from Little's law; supermarkets and kanban loops sized. Push versus pull honestly compared. Where pull fails: long changeovers and unstable processes.
preview: What does Little's law say about batch size and lead time?
When does pull genuinely fail?

lessons.scope: Internal versus external setup separated, then converted. Video-study method. Changeover time as capacity: the filling-line flavor change worked as the example.
preview: Internal versus external setup: give two conversions on a filling line.
Why does changeover time equal capacity?

lessons.scope: Workplace organization as failure prevention, not housekeeping theater. Visual controls that surface abnormality in seconds. The maintenance-shop 5S case: tools, spares, and shadow boards.
preview: What does 5S prevent, mechanically, in a maintenance shop?
What makes a visual control actually visual?

lessons.scope: Problem statements with baselines and scope; measurement-system checks before data is trusted — gauge R&R recalled. Process capability as the Measure phase's verdict. Projects chartered so they can fail honestly.
preview: What must a charter's baseline contain before Measure starts?
Why does gauge R&R precede data collection?

lessons.scope: Root-cause candidates from fishbones and stratification; hypothesis tests separating signal from noise. Regression on process data. The statistics course earning its keep.
preview: Stratify downtime by shift and product: what pattern would implicate process, not people?
Which test compares two shifts' means honestly?

lessons.scope: Solutions tested by experiment where stakes justify it; error-proofing over exhortation. Control plans and response plans that outlive the project team. The handover that decides whether gains persist.
preview: Error-proofing versus retraining: why does one persist?
What does a control plan hand to the process owner?

lessons.scope: Two-level factorials run on a process; effects and interactions computed and plotted. Blocking and randomization as insurance. A sealing-parameter experiment worked end to end.
preview: Design a 2² experiment on sealing: factors, response, and blocking.
What does randomization insure against?

lessons.scope: Wrench-time study as the maintenance value-stream map; travel, waiting, and parts-chasing quantified. Kitting and planning as waste removal. The improvement lens turned on the maintenance function itself.
preview: What does a wrench-time study typically find, and what absorbs the hours?
Which lean tool attacks parts-chasing directly?

lessons.scope: A guided DMAIC on a provided dataset: charter to control plan with tollgate reviews. Statistical tools used where they earn their place. The portfolio artifact for interviews.
preview: In the simulation, which tollgate nearly killed the project, and why?
What survived to the control plan?

courses.title: Engineering Economics & Project Management
courses.summary: Money and schedules as engineering quantities: time value, NPV and payback, replacement decisions, and the project management that delivers plant work — the language of every budget meeting you will attend.
taught_from: NPTEL: Engineering Economics and Project Management courses (nptel.ac.in)

lessons.scope: Interest, compounding, and equivalence; cash-flow diagrams drawn before any formula. Present and future worth conversions. The arithmetic under every 'is it worth it'.
preview: Draw the cash-flow diagram for a pump replacement with salvage.
Why does money have time value even without inflation?

lessons.scope: NPV, annual worth, and IRR with their agreement and disagreement cases. Do-nothing as a legitimate alternative. Ranking repairs, replacements, and upgrades on one axis.
preview: When do NPV and IRR disagree, and which do you trust?
Why is do-nothing always an alternative?

lessons.scope: Simple and discounted payback computed; where payback misleads and why management loves it anyway. Presenting both payback and NPV without contradiction. The reliability project's financial one-pager.
preview: What does payback ignore that NPV counts?
Present both without contradiction: how?

lessons.scope: Depreciation methods in outline and their cash-flow effects. Book versus market value in replacement studies. Enough tax logic to read finance's spreadsheet.
preview: Book versus market value: which enters a replacement study?
What does depreciation shield, cash-wise?

lessons.scope: Defender versus challenger economics; economic life from rising O&M against falling capital recovery. The repair-versus-replace decision formalized. A pump replacement case argued with numbers.
preview: Define economic life in one sentence.
Why does rising O&M eventually beat falling capital recovery?

lessons.scope: Acquisition, ownership, and disposal costs integrated; energy and maintenance dominating most industrial LCCs. Sensitivity on the assumptions that matter. Procurement decisions moved from price to cost.
preview: Which two costs dominate industrial LCC, and what does that do to procurement?
What sensitivity check makes an LCC credible?

lessons.scope: Sensitivity analysis and breakeven on the variables that actually move a decision. Expected value with honest probabilities; decision trees for repair-versus-replace under uncertainty. Presenting ranges instead of false precision — the analyst's credibility insurance.
preview: What does a sensitivity chart reveal that a single NPV number hides?
When is expected value the wrong basis for a one-shot decision?

lessons.scope: Scope, WBS, and responsibility assignment; the triple constraint stated without cliché. Stakeholders and the single-page charter. Plant projects sized from kilodinar to shutdown scale.
preview: What does a WBS decompose, and what must its leaves support?
Why does scope creep start at the charter?

lessons.scope: Networks, critical paths, and float computed by hand once. Crashing economics. The schedule literacy turnarounds will demand at scale.
preview: Compute float on a simple network: what does zero float mark?
What does crashing trade?

lessons.scope: Budgets, commitments, and actuals separated; earned-value indices computed and interpreted. Forecast at completion honestly. Progress reporting that survives audit.
preview: Interpret CPI 0.85 and SPI 1.1 together.
Why do commitments, not just actuals, matter in cost control?

lessons.scope: Build the full case for one reliability improvement: baseline losses, options, NPV/payback, risks, and implementation schedule. Presented as a one-pager plus backup. The document that gets funding.
preview: In your business case, which assumption moved NPV most?
What implementation risk did you price, and how?
